headline: Iranian Kurdish fighters in Iraq hope Tehran's 'hated dictatorship' will fall
subhead: Between 4,000 and 5,000 Iranian Kurdish fighters are based in Iraqi Kurdistan. They have high hopes for the US-Israeli operation in Iran.

headline: Far right lays trap for Dati in Paris mayoral race
subhead: Rachida Dati, the conservative candidate for mayor of Paris, finds herself in an uncomfortable position as Eric Zemmour's party calls for an alliance in the second round.

headline: From 9 am to 9 pm, six days a week: The rise of '996' work culture in French start-ups
subhead: Driven by the race for artificial intelligence, some French start-ups now value total employee commitment and constant availability as key conditions for success.

headline: PSG is struggling but not yet out of the race
subhead: As Paris Saint-Germain (PSG) faces Chelsea on Wednesday in the round of 16 of the Champions League, the Parisian club has lost the dominance it displayed last season. Nevertheless, the current campaign is far from over, writes journalist Jérôme Latta.

headline: 'The tendency to treat a local election as a national test may seem surprising'
subhead: With just 14 months to go before the 2027 presidential election, France's municipal elections are seen as a major political test by candidates seeking momentum, explains Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz.

Madagascar's leader fires PM, suspends government
The ghosts of the new Chilean president's family
subhead: José Antonio Kast, who will be sworn in on Wednesday, makes no secret of his admiration for Augusto Pinochet's regime. He is less forthcoming about his father's past as a former Wehrmacht soldier and his family's role during the atrocities committed in Paine during the 1973 coup.

headline: The United Arab Emirates is caught in the fog of war
subhead: Surprised by the scale of Iran's attacks on their territory, Emirati leaders have worked to downplay the impact in order to retain the confidence of investors and tourists, while continuing to place orders for weapons.

headline: Fashion's brand ambassadors step into an age of emancipation
subhead: For a long time, contracts between stars and fashion houses forbade them from appearing dressed in a rival label. Today, thanks to awards ceremonies and prestigious premieres, a new trend of increasing collaborations is emerging, shaking up the relationship between luxury and celebrities.

headline: Dominique Eddé, Lebanese writer: 'The present moment is overwhelming; it is one of destruction. All that remains is to think and to stand tall'
Israel and war as the only prospect
'War is far too sensitive an issue to be managed by artificial intelligence'
